428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-s660 . Fax (916) 322-0886

C-4,204up
C)tralinga, CA93210
Re: Our File No. G-06-035
Dear Mr. Plitt:
We have received additional correspondence from you requesting that we direct
you to information on Califbrnia's "Three Strikes Law." As we advised you in previous
<;orrespondence, your request is not within the purview of the Fair Political Practices
Commission. Therefore, we are unable to further assist you.
Sincerely,
Menchaca
y:

I:\Adviceltrs\06-035


Fern Polrrrcel PRACrrcEs CouurssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 10,2006
Bryant W. Plitt
7-91874
Post Office Box 8503
C-4,204up
Coalinga, CA932l0
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-06-035
Dear Mr. Plitt:
This letter is in response to your request regarding Assembly BillNo. 50 (2005-
2006 Reg. Sess.) as it relates to California's "Three Strikes Law."
QUESTTON
Your questions pertain to the role the Fair Political Practices Commission
("FPPC') has in the passage of Assembly Bill 50 (Leno), which if passed by the
Legislature, will amend sections of the Califomia Penal Code regarding sexual offenders
ANALYSIS
The role of the FPPC in state government is addressed on our web site as follows:
The Mission of the FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION is to promote the integrity of representative state
and local govemment in California through fair, impartial
interpretation and enforcement of political campaign, lobbying, and
conflict of interest laws.
(Emphasis added.)
tfrtw3


FileNo. G-06-035
Page No. 2
The Fair Political Practices Commission was created by the Political Reform Act
(the "Act")t of 1974, a ballot initiative passed by voters. The FPPC investigates alleged
violations of the Political Reform Act, and enforces conflict of interest codes.
Unfortunately, your question regarding AB 50 does not fall within the purview of the
Act.
I encourage you to direct your comments regarding SB 50 to the state legislative
representatives in your district. I would also advise you to monitor a proposed ballot
initiative entitled, "The Three Strikes Reform Act of 2006," whichhas been filed for title
and summary with the California Office of the Attorney General by Los Angeles District
Attomey Steve Cooley and Brian Dunn, Esquire.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Generdl Counsel
By: Joan Giannetta
Legal Assistant, Legal
JG/rd
I:\Adviceltrs\06-035
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, ofthe California Code of Regulations.
